channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Analysis: Three months in, is Trump losing the Iran war?
item.description: Despite winning nearly every battle against Iran, Trump faces growing doubts about whether he can translate tactical successes into a geopolitical win, with Iran still controlling the Strait of Hormuz and resisting nuclear concessions.

item.title: Shein to buy apparel retailer Everlane
item.description: Fast-fashion giant Shein announced an agreement to acquire US apparel brand Everlane, known for sustainability and supply-chain transparency, marking a significant expansion into Western retail.

item.title: Indonesia working on scheme to import 150M barrels of Russian oil
item.description: Indonesia is developing a special import framework to fulfill its plan to import 150 million barrels of Russian crude this year, though only one shipment has reached the country so far.

item.title: Ukraine attacks Russian oil refinery in Yaroslavl, Zelenskiy says
item.description: Ukrainian forces struck a Russian oil refinery 700 km from the Ukrainian border, the latest in Kyiv's ongoing campaign targeting Russian energy infrastructure.

item.title: Xi calls for all-out rescue after coal mine explosion kills over 80 in China
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping demanded an investigation and accountability after a gas explosion at a Shanxi Province coal mine killed at least 82 people, with rescue operations ongoing.

item.title: US Green Card Applicants Must Return Home First Under New Trump Policy
item.description: US Citizenship and Immigration Services announced that foreigners seeking permanent residency must now apply from their home countries, ending a provision that allowed applications from within the US. The policy could affect hundreds of thousands of applicants annually.

item.title: Israel Exposes UAE Defense Ties With Iron Dome Deployment Reveal
item.description: US Ambassador to Israel Mike Huckabee inadvertently exposed UAE-Israel defense cooperation by confirming Iron Dome battery deployments. The disclosure puts Abu Dhabi in an awkward diplomatic position after months of denying Iranian accusations of military collaboration.

item.title: Taiwan Faces Mixed Signals as Trump Warning Meets Historic Arms Offer
item.description: A proposed $8 billion arms package discussion between Washington and Taipei collides with Trump statement that he is not looking for anyone to go independent, creating policy whiplash for the island amid heightened cross-strait tensions.

item.title: Slovenia parliament approves right-wing Janez Jansa as prime minister
item.description: Slovenia's parliament approved the return of populist former PM Janez Jansa, marking a sharp rightward shift for the EU member state previously governed by liberals.

item.title: France presidential race sees ex-PM Philippe as early favorite to beat populists
item.description: Latest polls suggest center-right figure Philippe is the only candidate who can defeat Marine Le Pen or Jean-Luc Melenchon in Frances next presidential election.

item.title: US tells temporary visa holders to leave country to apply for green cards
item.description: New policy closes loophole allowing people to apply for permanent residency while in the US, forcing foreigners to apply from abroad.

item.title: Canada Alberta referendum on independence unlikely to succeed, analysts say
item.description: Albertas separatist referendum is unlikely to pass, but the vote reflects growing frustration in Western Canada with federal policies and energy sector decline.

item.title: Turkey arrests hundreds for opposing the Iran war, media reports say
item.description: Turkish authorities detained more than 400 people for social media posts criticizing the government stance on the Iran conflict, highlighting domestic tensions over Ankaras neutrality policy.

item.title: Russia faces domestic pressure as Ukraine war enters fourth year
item.description: Domestic discontent is rising in Russia over military casualties and economic sanctions, with regional elections showing declining support for United Russia in several areas.

item.title: At least 8 miners killed, 38 trapped after coal mine collapses in northern China
item.description: A gas explosion at a coal mine in Shanxi Province killed at least eight workers with dozens more trapped underground, the latest in a series of deadly mining accidents in China.

item.title: Mexico and EU launch new chapter in relations with modern global agreement
item.description: Mexico and the European Union signed an updated global agreement deepening economic and political ties, signaling a strategic partnership amid shifting global trade dynamics.

item.title: UK conducting wargames for AI-driven NATO conflict with Russia
item.description: The UK military is running advanced simulations using artificial intelligence to prepare for potential conflict scenarios with Russia, amid heightened tensions in Europe.

item.title: UN nuclear non-proliferation treaty meeting stumbles as US and Iran clash
item.description: A United Nations conference on nuclear non-proliferation faced deadlock as US and Iranian delegations exchanged accusations over Tehrans uranium enrichment program amid ongoing conflict.

item.title: World Bank document shows 27 countries seeking access to crisis funds
item.description: Twenty-seven nations have approached the World Bank for emergency financing amid global economic turmoil driven by the Iran conflict and energy price spikes.

item.title: Investigation into King Charles brother Andrew will be lengthy, UK police say
item.description: British police said an investigation into Prince Andrew would take considerable time following allegations that have resurfaced about the Duke of York.

item.title: Pakistan seeks breakthrough in US-Iran peace talks
item.description: Pakistans foreign minister is mediating between Washington and Tehran as both sides remain deadlocked over Irans uranium stockpile and control of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: France readies UN resolution on Hormuz as US text stalls
item.description: France is preparing a UN Security Council resolution on Hormuz passage after a US-backed proposal failed to gain sufficient support from council members.

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as Trumps National Intelligence Director
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard has stepped down as Director of National Intelligence after roughly a year in the role, Fox News Digital reported, with no immediate successor named.

item.title: Trump identifies potential military targets in Iran for next strikes
item.description: US President Donald Trump has identified specific Iranian facilities that could be targeted if diplomatic efforts fail, as negotiations continue via Pakistani mediators.

item.title: Israeli strike kills two on south Lebanon military site
item.description: An Israeli airstrike killed two people at a military installation in southern Lebanon, continuing cross-border tensions despite ongoing diplomatic efforts in the region.
